risks: No managed identity resource explicitly defined — identity-based auth is configured on the ML workspace (systemDatastoresAuthMode: Identity) but no User-Assigned Managed Identity resource exists in the plan to facilitate cross-service access
ACR encryption status is set to 'enabled' but lacks accompanying keyVaultProperties and identity configuration — deployment will require these values to be provided at IaC authoring time
No resource tagging strategy defined for cost allocation, environment classification, or compliance tracking across the seven resources
No explicit diagnostic settings resources to route resource-level platform logs (e.g., Storage, Key Vault, ACR audit logs) to the Log Analytics workspace
Standard_DS3_v2 (4 vCPU, 14 GB RAM) may be undersized for production ML training workloads — evaluate actual model requirements
Key Vault access model not specified (enableRbacAuthorization) — RBAC model is recommended over access policies per Azure best practices
No backup or disaster recovery strategy documented for Key Vault secrets, ML workspace metadata, or trained model artifacts
correctionsRecommended: Add a Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities resource and reference it from the ML workspace, ACR, and storage account for identity-based access

Complete ACR encryption configuration by adding keyVaultProperties (keyIdentifier referencing a Key Vault key) and an identity reference, or remove the encryption.status flag if platform-managed encryption is sufficient
Add a resource tagging convention (e.g., environment, project, costCenter, owner) to all resources for governance and cost management
Add Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings resources for Storage, Key Vault, and Container Registry to route platform logs to the Log Analytics workspace for comprehensive audit coverage
Set enableRbacAuthorization: true on Key Vault to use Azure RBAC instead of access policies, aligning with Azure security best practices
Evaluate compute SKU sizing against actual training workload requirements — consider GPU-enabled SKUs (e.g., Standard_NC series) if training deep learning models
Consider adding an Azure Monitor action group and alert rules for training job failures and resource health monitoring
